meta_rule: The addition is indistinguishable in style from a senior human's edit to the surrounding file.

severity_model.BLOCKER: Blocks merge ONLY when confidence:high and the category is block_eligible. Emitted as an in-source CRAFT-FIX marker.
severity_model.MAJOR: Non-blocking inline review comment. Tracked for calibration.

rules.title: Over-commenting
rules.rule: Comments explain why, never what. No comment that restates the code. No docstring on a self-evident function. Match the surrounding file's comment density.

rules.title: Defensive-programming noise
rules.rule: No redundant nil check or re-validation of an input already validated upstream or guaranteed by the type. Errors flow via crm/errs sentinels; handlers return them, the choke-point maps them. No swallowing errors into generic logs.

rules.title: Over-engineering / premature abstraction
rules.rule: No interface, factory, generic, or base type without a second concrete caller today (YAGNI). A 20-line job is a function, not a hierarchy. The Mat-Ryer service shape is the ceiling of ceremony, not the floor.

rules.title: Textbook uniformity
rules.rule: Names read like the domain (deal, passport, captureRun), not CS-textbook filler (data, result, tmp, helper, manager, processData). No verbose identifiers where the package already gives context.

rules.title: Style drift
rules.rule: Naming, formatting, error-wrapping, file structure, and test shape match the module the change lands in. Compare every hunk against its surrounding code, not an abstract ideal.

rules.title: Type escape hatches
rules.rule: No Go interface{}/any or unchecked type assertion, no TS any/as/@ts-ignore, no //nolint to dodge a real type or lint fix. Fix the type. A genuine any at a serialization boundary is justified in the PR, not silently.

rules.title: Surface polish over substance
rules.rule: Edge cases handled honestly: the empty list, the timezone boundary, the concurrent write (If-Match/version), the cross-tenant query (RLS). A PR that looks complete but drops the hard case is slop.

rules.title: Dead / speculative code
rules.rule: No commented-out blocks, no unreferenced for-future-use exports, no TODO without an issue ref. The diff contains only what this change needs.

rules.title: Untrustworthy dependencies
rules.rule: No new dependency without justification in the PR; no import of a package absent from the lockfile (hallucination / slopsquatting guard). Prefer the stdlib and the existing gw-* libraries.

rules.title: Oversized, unexplained PRs
rules.rule: The change is scoped and tells a story. A huge PR the author cannot explain line by line is the canonical rejected case; PR hygiene is in scope.

rules.title: Idiomatic to this codebase
rules.rule: Use the crm/errs sentinels, the Mat-Ryer service shape, ctx first, provenance-on-write, the seam-vs-internal rule; leave generated _gen.go files untouched.

rules.title: Small, focused, legible
rules.rule: Prefer many small files over a few large; the ~400-500-line norm is the smell threshold. One concept per file.

rules.title: Tests read as specifications
rules.rule: Pure-unit mapping tests favored; table-driven where it clarifies; names state the behavior, not the mechanics; tests cover the honest edge cases of T7.

rules.title: The PR tells a story
rules.rule: A title that names the change, a body that says what/why/how-verified, a commit history a reviewer can follow. Conventional, present-tense commit subjects.

rules.title: Restraint
rules.rule: The best diff is often the smallest. Deleting code, reusing an existing helper, or picking the one opinionated default beats adding a clever new abstraction.
